page: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is a memory-optimized virtual machine designed for workloads requiring massive RAM capacity and high-core compute density. Powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor running at 2.7 GHz, this instance features 208 dedicated vCPUs mapped across 104 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 5888.0 GB (5.75 TB) of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 28.31 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent data.

Benchmark results highlight a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core performance. While single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25%, the massive core count drives top-tier multi-core performance. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Memory performance is highly robust, with cached read-only small block bandwidth reaching the top 10% at 10166.66 GB/sec, and large-block read/write bandwidth ranking in the top 25%. Additionally, the server demonstrates exceptional throughput in Redis SET operations, placing in the top 10% with 147.67 million operations per second, and strong static web serving throughput.

Given its specialized hardware profile, the m2-ultramem-208 represents a high-density resource allocation tailored for enterprise-scale in-memory databases, such as SAP HANA, large-scale Redis deployments, and complex data warehousing. While the single-threaded performance is modest, the massive memory capacity and high multi-threaded throughput make it highly cost-efficient for consolidating memory-bound workloads that would otherwise require multi-node clustering. It is also well-suited for heavy database operations, software compilation, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 208 dedicated vCPUs (104 physical Intel Xeon cores at 2.7 GHz) and 5888.0 GB of RAM. Designed for memory-bound enterprise workloads, it lacks local storage and GPUs. While single-core benchmarks are weak, its multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities are top-tier, with stress-ng multi-core and cached memory read bandwidth ranking in the top 10%. It is optimized for large in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and heavy data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is a memory-optimized server with 208 vCPUs and 5.75 TB of RAM. It delivers top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for enterprise in-memory databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is a memory-optimized server featuring 208 vCPUs and 5.75 TB of RAM, designed for large-scale in-memory databases.
tagline: A massive memory-optimized instance featuring 5.75 TB of RAM and 208 vCPUs for enterprise in-memory databases and data warehousing.

5888.0 GB (5.75 TB) of RAM with 28.31 GB per core
